summary: Auto-chess (自走棋) game with ancient Egyptian war theme and Egyptian elements
architectural_notes: Battle board grid should use regular hexagons instead of squares/hex approximations
Visual style reference: Iron Command (钢铁命令) — tactical/strategic war game aesthetics for overall layout and UI presentation
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Aerial night-view Egyptian battlefield with dark sandy terrain, moonlit dune contours, faint hieroglyph map markings, and a tactical grid overlay texture (battle-board-dark-bg.dim_1920x1080.png)
- Aged bronze and ember-gold command panel border frame with riveted corner details and worn parchment center texture (command-panel-frame-dark.dim_960x640.png)

existing_features.title: Implement a full auto-chess game loop: a preparation phase where the player buys and places Egyptian units on a hex/grid board, and a combat phase where units automatically battle enemy units. Include rounds, a shop system with random unit offerings, gold economy (earn gold per round, interest mechanic), player health points that decrease on losses, and a game-over condition.

Create a roster of at least 8 distinct Egyptian-themed unit types: Pharaoh (legendary melee tank), Anubis Guard (melee fighter), Ra Priest (ranged magical attacker), Isis Sorceress (healer/support), Charioteer (fast ranged), Sphinx (area-damage caster), Horus Archer (ranged DPS), and Mummy Warrior (undead melee). Each unit has name, portrait icon, tier (1–3 star upgrade), attack, HP, ability description, and an Egyptian faction/synergy tag.

Implement a synergy/trait system with at least 4 Egyptian-themed synergies: 'Children of Ra' (Priests — bonus magic damage), 'Pharaoh's Guard' (Warriors — bonus armor), 'Desert Nomads' (Charioteers — bonus speed), 'Undead Legion' (Mummies — HP regeneration). Activating synergies at threshold counts (e.g., 2/4 units) grants passive buffs to all matching units.

Build a 2D top-down battle board using an 8×4 hex or square grid. Animate units moving toward enemies and attacking during the combat phase. Show floating damage numbers, HP bars above units, and death animations (units fade/crumble). Use Egyptian-themed visual effects: golden glow for Ra abilities, sand particle bursts for desert units, hieroglyph flash for magical spells.

Design the entire UI with an ancient Egyptian aesthetic: sandy desert color palette (gold, sand beige, turquoise, dark ochre), hieroglyph-style decorative borders on panels, papyrus-textured card backgrounds, and Egyptian iconography (scarab, Eye of Horus, ankh, pyramid silhouettes) used as decorative elements throughout the interface. Use a serif or hieroglyph-inspired font for headings.

Create a main menu screen with the game title 'Pharaoh's Conquest', a Start Game button, and a How to Play modal explaining the auto-chess rules. Include a round tracker HUD showing current round number, player HP, current gold, and the active synergies panel during gameplay.

Render the generated Egyptian-themed images as static assets and integrate them into the UI: use the desert background on the main menu and battle board, the Egyptian panel border frame on all game panels, and unit portrait icons on unit cards and the shop.

Audit and fix the App.tsx root component so it renders the full Pharaoh's Conquest game (MainMenu → GameHUD + BattleBoard + Shop + SynergyPanel + Bench) instead of the legacy quiz/ecosystem layout. Remove all references to Header, Quiz, Footer, and any non-game components from App.tsx.
Fix the HTML entry point (frontend/index.html) to set the page title to 'Pharaoh's Conquest' in English instead of the current Chinese ecosystem simulation title.

Implement or complete the MainMenu screen component (if not already present as a standalone file) that shows the game title 'Pharaoh's Conquest' with Egyptian-styled typography, a Start Game button, and a How to Play button that opens HowToPlayModal. The background should use the desert background image asset.

Ensure all empty stub components (ControlPanel.tsx, GameScene.tsx, StatsPanel.tsx, Terrain.tsx, PlantMesh.tsx, AnimalMesh.tsx) are either removed from the project or replaced with meaningful Egyptian auto-chess implementations if they are referenced by the game UI. Remove any component files that belong to prior unrelated apps.

Verify that unit portrait images at the paths defined in frontend/src/data/units.ts are correctly mapped to the generated static assets in frontend/public/assets/generated. Update any broken portrait paths so every unit card and shop card displays its correct Egyptian unit portrait.

Replace the current square grid cells in BattleBoard.tsx and GridTile.tsx with regular hexagons (pointy-top or flat-top orientation). Implement a proper hex grid layout for the 7×4 (player) and 7×4 (enemy) sections using offset or axial coordinates. Hexagon tiles should use CSS clip-path or SVG polygon to render the correct six-sided shape. Unit sprites, HP bars, and drag-and-drop logic must be adapted to work on the hexagonal grid. Gaps between hex tiles should use standard hex grid spacing so tiles tessellate correctly.

Overhaul the overall visual presentation of the game inspired by the Iron Command (钢铁命令) real-time strategy aesthetic: a gritty, high-contrast war-room look with sharp panel edges, tactical overlay styling, and a darker base palette (deep desert night tones — dark sand, aged bronze, deep lapis, ember gold) contrasted by bright unit highlight colors. The battle board should feel like a tactical command map: add a subtle grid coordinate label system (column letters, row numbers) along the board edges, a dark vignette border around the board, and unit tokens that look like military unit markers with bold iconography. The HUD should be redesigned to resemble a tactical command interface with segmented stat blocks (HP, Gold, Round, Streak) displayed in sharp bordered panels. Shop cards should adopt a compact military-briefing card style. All panels (SynergyPanel, Shop, Bench, HUD) should use dark backgrounds with gold and bronze accent borders. Typography for labels and stats should shift to a monospaced or condensed sans-serif for stat numbers while headings retain the Egyptian serif font.

Update the global Tailwind CSS design tokens in frontend/src/index.css and frontend/tailwind.config.js to introduce the new dark tactical palette: add CSS custom properties for deep-sand-dark, aged-bronze, ember-gold, deep-lapis-dark, and command-panel background colors. Ensure these tokens are applied consistently across all game components updated in REQ-14.

Generate a new dark tactical desert battle board background image to replace or supplement the existing desert background. The image should evoke a night-time aerial view of an Egyptian battlefield with a dark sandy terrain, subtle moonlit dune contours, faint hieroglyph map markings, and a tactical grid overlay texture. Use dimensions 1920×1080px. Also generate an updated dark command panel frame/border image for the game panels, with aged bronze and ember-gold tones, riveted corner details, and worn parchment texture. Use dimensions 960×640px.

new_feature_requests.summary: Auto-chess game with ancient Egyptian war theme featuring Egyptian units, elements, and aesthetics
Change grid cells to regular hexagons and optimize the visual presentation inspired by Iron Command (钢铁命令) aesthetics
